behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both the file and the environment, the error message names the missing key by its exact config path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is config-file parse + env overlay + required-key validation + error formatting, so a single-function Unit test can miss handoff bugs (MOCK-MISS). No real network/subprocess/OS-specific primitive is required; a temp file and process env are sufficient, so System is rejected by behavior text about startup config loading only (REJECTION-GATE). No AI output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run loader against a temp config file missing `database.host` and with `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST` unset; assert startup fails and emitted error contains exact path `database.host`.

behaviors.description: When the environment variable `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, the loader resolves the config file path relative to that directory rather than `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is specifically about loader wiring between environment lookup and path resolution at startup; testing only a pure path helper risks missing integration with loader entrypoint (MOCK-MISS). Real OS/network/subprocess behavior is not required: setting env and using temp directories is enough, so System is rejected by text limited to path selection logic (REJECTION-GATE). No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp directory containing a config file, ensure `~/.config/tool/config.yaml` is absent/different, run loader, and assert it reads from `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/tool/config.yaml`.

behaviors.description: When an environment variable with the `TOOL_` prefix conflicts with a key in the config file, the environment variable value is used.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module boundary where file-derived values and env-derived overrides are merged with precedence rules (MOCK-MISS). Unit-testing merge logic alone may miss mapping/wiring bugs from `TOOL_` names to config paths. No real OS/network/subprocess need beyond normal env and local file setup, so System is disqualified by behavior text focused on precedence semantics (REJECTION-GATE). No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create config file with a value (e.g., `database.host: filehost`), set conflicting `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST=envhost`, run loader, and assert effective config uses `envhost`.

behaviors.description: When the config file contains a YAML syntax error, the error message includes the line number where the problem was detected.
behaviors.justification: Need real interaction between YAML parser exception metadata and CLI error rendering. Mocking parser can hide real line-number formats/fields (MOCK-HIDE). Still no external network/subprocess; local malformed file + real YAML library call is sufficient, so System is rejected because behavior text is about parse/report content, not OS-level conditions (REJECTION-GATE). No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Write malformed YAML with known bad line into temp config, invoke loader, assert failure message includes that line number from parser diagnostics.

behaviors.description: When the config directory does not exist at startup, the loader creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans startup orchestration + filesystem adapter + defaults writer, so module handoff is the likely failure boundary (MOCK-MISS). Local filesystem interaction is required, but the behavior text only requires create/write under normal conditions; no subprocess, real network, or OS-specific event semantics are stated, so System is considered but rejected (REJECTION-GATE). No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Point config home to a temp path with no existing config directory, run startup loader, assert directory is created, defaults file exists with expected baseline keys, and loader continues successfully.
